title: Operators should be overloaded consistently
description: Operators should be overloaded consistentlyAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen overloading some arithmetic operator overloads, it is very important to make sure that all related operators and methods are consistent in their implementation.The following guidelines should be followed:When providingoperator ==, !=you should also provideEquals(Object)andGetHashCode().When providingoperator +, -, *, / or %you should also provideoperator ==, respecting the previous guideline.This rule raises an issue when any of these guidelines are not followed on a publicly-visible class or struct (public,protectedorprotected internal).Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOp
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: Classes that implement both == and != operators
Classes that override Equals and GetHashCode
logic.reasoning: Detects classes that implement == operator but may be missing != operator or Equals/GetHashCode overrides. Equality operators should be implemented consistently.

should_trigger: public static bool operator ==(MyClass a, MyClass b) { }
